source.title: Diversity is All You Need: Learning Skills without a Reward Function

confidence_rationale: DIAYN supplies the missing reward-agnostic option-discovery algorithm: skills are discovered by maximising mutual information between skill index z and visited states under a maximum-entropy policy. This bridges ARC-065 (diversity generation) and ARC-064 (bottom-up rule discovery) via a single information-theoretic objective and is therefore directly relevant to the Pull 1 + Pull 2 cross-walk. Confidence not higher because (a) DIAYN skills are typically held fixed during downstream RL rather than continually re-discovered, (b) skill diversity is enforced via discriminator entropy which can be brittle in continuous-state environments, and (c) the empirical demonstrations are simulated robotics (HalfCheetah, Ant, etc.), not biological agents.
failure_signatures: If REE's MECH-313 (stochastic-noise-floor) and MECH-316 (cross-episode-regularity-extraction) cannot be unified into a single mutual-information objective like DIAYN's, the architectural-economy claim that ARC-065 and ARC-064 are one machinery in two readings is weakened.

DIAYN's discriminator-collapse failure mode (degenerate skills the discriminator cannot distinguish) is directly homologous to the option-collapse and monomodal-collapse patterns REE has hit; the mitigation literature transfers.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Skills can be learned without a reward function by maximising the mutual information I(s; z) between skill index z and visited states s, under a maximum-entropy policy that maximises H[A | S, z] (entropy over actions conditional on state and skill). The result is a diverse set of skills that can be composed for downstream tasks. The information-theoretic objective unifies skill-diversification (high mutual information across skills) and within-skill exploration (high action entropy).
mapping.ree_translation: DIAYN provides a single objective that simultaneously generates behavioural diversity (ARC-065 territory) AND extracts a discriminable set of skills from that diversity (ARC-064 territory). For REE this is potentially transformative: the proposed split between MECH-313 (stochastic-noise-floor, ARC-065) and MECH-316 (cross-episode-regularity-extraction, ARC-064) might be implementable as one objective rather than two separate substrates. The DIAYN discriminator that decodes skill from state is also a clean computational analog of MECH-318 (rule-state-abstraction-substrate, OFC-as-cognitive-map): an explicit machinery that hosts state-to-skill labels.
mapping.mapping_caveat: DIAYN's information-theoretic objective enforces skill discrimination via state visitation; this works well in environments where different skills naturally produce different state distributions, but degenerates in environments where multiple skills can produce similar states. REE's grid-world substrate may or may not have enough state-space variety to support DIAYN-style discrimination. Also: DIAYN typically learns a fixed-size set of skills offline and then re-uses them downstream; REE's continual-discovery use case is closer to never-resting option-critic than to canonical DIAYN. The mapping is conceptually faithful but the operational regime differs.
mapping.source_context: ICLR 2019 conference paper (preprint 2018). Empirical demonstrations on simulated robotics (HalfCheetah, Ant, Walker2D, Hopper) and 2D mazes. No biological data.

confidence_components.notes: Top-tier ML venue (ICLR), 4000+ citations, code released, replicated. Mapping fidelity is high for the cross-walk between ARC-065 and ARC-064 because DIAYN literally unifies them in one objective. Transfer risk moderate because the simulated-robotics empirical regime and offline-then-reuse training schedule do not match REE's continual-online substrate.
